name: resilient-host
description: Set up and verify a reboot/power-loss-resilient Claude Code remote-control host on Linux or macOS. Linux: a systemd user service that runs `claude --remote-control` inside a dedicated tmux PTY with Restart=always and an API-reachability pre-check, user linger so it starts at boot with no login, SSH enabled, GRUB hidden/zero-timeout so unclean boots don't pause, and a BIOS power-on rule. macOS: a launchd LaunchAgent (RunAtLoad + KeepAlive) that brings the tmux `claude --remote-control` session back on every login, with pmset power-on, Remote Login, and FileVault/Keychain caveats for unattended boot. Each failure mode (power outage, bootloader pause, session crash, no-login reboot) maps to a distinct layer. Privileged steps are owner-run sudo blocks. OS/kernel-hang recovery (a hardware watchdog) is intentionally out of scope for now and will be added later; the skill does not claim the host survives a kernel hang.
